front: Artist: Belgian painter; founding member of Les XX (Les Vingt), organized by critic Octave Maus
Artist: The Oyster Eater (depicting his sister Mitche eating oysters) was rejected by L'Essor AND Antwerp Salon — prompting formation of Les XX
Artist: later split with Les XX over their adoption of pointillism (Seurat/Signac)
Artist: mother owned a curio shop whose oriental vases and dolls appear in his paintings
Artist: in Self-Portrait with Masks, the only unmasked face among grotesque masked heads; wears a red feathered hat
Artist: self-portrait imagining himself at age 100, menaced by a spider on a divan
Painting: satirizes critics as cooks — depicts Octave Maus and Edmond Picard as the title figures
Painting: skeletons over a stove bearing the phrase 'no fire; will you have any tomorrow?'
Painting: skeletons form a triangle with a man wearing a 'civet' sign (body of a hanged man)
back: Skeletons Fighting for the Body of a Hanged Man (James Ensor)

front: Painting: Death draped in a white cloak and floppy red hat, surrounded by masked figures
Painting: depicts a monkey's crucifixion
Painting: in Scandalized Masks, the artist depicts his grandmother entering a room where his alcoholic father sits despondent
Painting: Russian Music — portrait of Anna Boch, the only female member of Les XX
Artist: painted Christ and the Critics — an Ecce Homo scene depicting himself as Christ
